60.text: If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a disease and the agitators in the city do not desist, We shall most certainly set you over them, then they shall not be your neighbors in it but for a little while;

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: If the hypocrites and those who have tainted hearts and the scandal mongers of Madinah do not desist, We shall surely give you authority over them and their days in that city will be numbered.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: Truly, if the Hypocrites, and those in whose hearts is a disease, and those who stir up sedition in the City, desist not, We shall certainly stir thee up against them: Then will they not be able to stay in it as thy neighbours for any length of time:

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Lain lam yantahi almunafiqoona waallatheena fee quloobihim maradun waalmurjifoona fee almadeenati lanughriyannaka bihim thumma la yujawiroonaka feeha illa qaleelan

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: Accursed, they would be seized wherever found, and slain mercilessly.

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: Accursed – to be caught wherever found and to be slain thoroughly.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: cursed they shalt be, and wheresoever they are come upon they shall be seized and slaughtered all --

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Accursed; wherever found they shall be laid hold of and slain with a relentless slaughter.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: Accursed, wherever found, they shall be seized and killed with a (terrible) slaughter.

61.author: Itani
61.text: They are cursed; wherever they are found, they should be captured and killed outright.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: They shall be cursed from all around and they shall be ruthlessly killed wherever they are seized.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: Accursed, they shall be seized wherever found, and killed with a (terrible) slaughter.

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Accursed, they will be seized wherever found and slain with a (fierce) slaughter.

61.author: Qarai
61.text: Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain violently:

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: cursed wherever they are found, they will be seized and put to death.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: Accursed wherever they are found, [being] seized and massacred completely.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: They will be condemned wherever they are and will be sized and done away with for good.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Cursed: wherever they are found they shall be seized and murdered, a (horrible) murdering.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: Accursed, wherever they are found, they will be seized and killed.

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: They shall have a curse on them: whenever they are found, they shall be seized and slain (without mercy).

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: MalAAooneena aynama thuqifoo okhithoo waquttiloo taqteelan

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: Such was the law of God among those before you; and you will not find any change in the law of God.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: The tradition of Allah prevalent among those who passed away before; and you will never find the traditions of Allah changing.

62.author: Arberry
62.text: God's wont with those who passed away before; and thou shall find no changing the wont of God.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: That hath been the dispensation of Allah with those who have passed away afore; and thou shalt not find in the dispensation of Allah any change.

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: That was the Way of Allah in the case of those who passed away of old, and you will not find any change in the Way of Allah.

62.author: Itani
62.text: Such has been God’s precedent with those who passed away before. You will find no change in God’s system.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: This has been Allah's Way with those who have gone before, and you shall find no change in Allah's Way.

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: That was the way of Allah in the case of those who passed away of old, and you will not find any change in the way of Allah.

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: That was the way of Allah in the case of those who passed away of old; thou wilt not find for the way of Allah aught of power to change.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: Such has been the way of Allah with those who have passed before them, and you shall find no change in the ways of Allah.

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: [This is] the established way of Allah with those who passed on before; and you will not find in the way of Allah any change.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: This was the tradition of God with those who lived before. There will never be any change in the tradition of God.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: (Such has been) the course of Allah with respect to those who have gone before; and you shall not find any change in the course of Allah.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: Such has been God's way with those who have gone before them. You shall find no change in the ways of God.

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: (Such was) the practice (approved) of Allah among those who lived aforetime: No change wilt thou find in the practice (approved) of Allah.

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Sunnata Allahi fee allatheena khalaw min qablu walan tajida lisunnati Allahi tabdeelan

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: People ask you about the Hour (of the great change). Say: "Only God has knowledge of it. Who knows? The Hour may be close at hand."

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: People ask you regarding the Last Day; proclaim, “Its knowledge is only with Allah; and what do you know, the Last Day may really be near!”

63.author: Arberry
63.text: The people will question thee concerning the Hour. Say: 'The knowledge of it is only with God; what shall make thee know? Haply the Hour is nigh.'

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: People ask thee ccncerning the Hour. Say thou: the knowledge there of is only with Allah: and what knowest thou! --belike the Hour is nigh.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: People ask you concerning the Hour, say: "The knowledge of it is with Allah only. What do you know? It may be that the Hour is near!"

63.author: Itani
63.text: The people ask you about the Hour. Say, “The knowledge thereof rests with God. But what do you know? Perhaps the hour is near.”

63.author: Maududi
63.text: People ask you concerning the Hour (of Resurrection). Say: “Allah alone has knowledge of it. What do you know? Perhaps the Hour is nigh.”

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: People ask you concerning the Hour, say: "The knowledge of it is with Allah only. What do you know It may be that the Hour is near!"

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Men ask thee of the Hour. Say: The knowledge of it is with Allah only. What can convey (the knowledge) unto thee? It may be that the Hour is nigh.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: The people question you concerning the Hour. Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah.’ What do you know, maybe the Hour is near.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: The people will ask you about the Hour. Say: 'The knowledge of it is with Allah alone, what makes you to know that the Hour is near'

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: People ask you concerning the Hour. Say," Knowledge of it is only with Allah. And what may make you perceive? Perhaps the Hour is near."

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: (Muhammad), people ask you about the Day of Judgment. Say, "Only God has knowledge about it. Perhaps the Hour of Doom will soon come to pass."

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Men ask you about the hour; say: The knowledge of it is only with Allah, and what will make you comprehend that the hour may be nigh.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: People will ask you about the Hour. Say, "God alone has knowledge of it. Who knows? The Hour may well be near at hand."

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Men ask thee concerning the Hour: Say, "The knowledge thereof is with Allah (alone)": and what will make thee understand?- perchance the Hour is nigh!

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Yasaluka alnnasu AAani alssaAAati qul innama AAilmuha AAinda Allahi wama yudreeka laAAalla alssaAAata takoonu qareeban

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: Verily God has cursed the infidels, and prepared a blazing fire for them.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: Allah has indeed cursed the disbelievers, and has kept prepared for them a blazing fire.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: God has cursed the unbelievers, and prepared for them a Blaze,

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: Verily Allah hath cursed the infidels, and hath gotten ready for them a Blaze.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: Verily, Allah has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a flaming Fire (Hell).

64.author: Itani
64.text: God has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a Blaze.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: Allah has cursed the unbelievers and has prepared for them a Blazing Fire;

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: Verily, Allah has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a flaming Fire.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Lo! Allah hath cursed the disbelievers, and hath prepared for them a flaming fire,

64.author: Qarai
64.text: Indeed Allah has cursed the faithless and prepared for them a blaze,

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: Allah has cursed the unbelievers and prepared for them a Blaze.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: Indeed, Allah has cursed the disbelievers and prepared for them a Blaze.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: God has condemned the unbelievers and prepared for them a burning torment

64.author: Shakir
64.text: Surely Allah has cursed the unbelievers and has prepared for them a burning fire,

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: God has rejected those who deny the truth and prepared for them a blazing Fire.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: Verily Allah has cursed the Unbelievers and prepared for them a Blazing Fire,-

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Inna Allaha laAAana alkafireena waaAAadda lahum saAAeeran

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: They will live in it for ever, and will find no saviour or helper.

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: In which they will remain forever; in it they will find neither a protector nor any supporter.

65.author: Arberry
65.text: therein to dwell for ever; they shall find neither protector nor helper.

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Abiders therein they shall be for ever, and they shall find neither a protecting friend nor a helper.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: Wherein they will abide for ever, and they will find neither a Wali (a protector) nor a helper.

65.author: Itani
65.text: Dwelling therein forever, not finding a protector or a savior.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: therein they shall abide for ever. They shall find none to protect or help them.

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: Wherein they will abide forever, and they will find neither a a protector nor a helper.

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: Wherein they will abide for ever. They will find (then) no protecting friend nor helper.

65.author: Qarai
65.text: in which they will remain forever, and will not find any guardian or helper.

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Living there for ever, they shall neither find a guardian nor a helper.

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Abiding therein forever, they will not find a protector or a helper.

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: wherein they will live forever without being able to find any guardian or helper.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: To abide therein for a long time; they shall not find a protector or a helper.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: There they will live forever, and they will find therein neither friend nor helper.

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: To dwell therein for ever: no protector will they find, nor helper.

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Khalideena feeha abadan la yajidoona waliyyan wala naseeran

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: The day their faces would be turned on the fire (as on a spit), they will say: "Alas! If only we had obeyed God, and obeyed the Prophet."

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: On the day when their faces will be overturned being roasted inside the fire, they will say, “Alas – if only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed His Noble Messenger!”

66.author: Arberry
66.text: Upon the day when their faces are turned about in the Fire they shall say, 'Ah, would we had obeyed God and the Messenger!

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: On the Day whereon their faces shall be rolled in the Fire, they will say: Oh that we had obeyed Allah and had obeyed the apostle!

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: On the Day when their faces will be turned over in the Fire, they will say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger (Muhammad SAW)."

66.author: Itani
66.text: The Day when their faces are flipped into the Fire, they will say, “If only we had obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger.”

66.author: Maududi
66.text: On that Day when their faces shall be turned around in the Fire, they will say: “Would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger.”

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: On the Day when their faces will be turned over in the Fire, they will say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger."

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: On the day when their faces are turned over in the Fire, they say: Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and had obeyed His messenger!

66.author: Qarai
66.text: The day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say, ‘We wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!’

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: On that Day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they shall say: 'Would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger'

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: The Day their faces will be turned about in the Fire, they will say, "How we wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger."

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: On the day when their faces will be turned from side to side on the fire, they will say, "Would that we had obeyed God and the Messenger!"

66.author: Shakir
66.text: On the day when their faces shall be turned back into the fire, they shall say: O would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: On the Day when their faces are turned over in the Fire, they shall say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed God, and obeyed the Messenger!"

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: The Day that their faces will be turned upside down in the Fire, they will say: "Woe to us! Would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger!"

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Yawma tuqallabu wujoohuhum fee alnnari yaqooloona ya laytana ataAAna Allaha waataAAna alrrasoola

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: They will say: "O our Lord, we obeyed our leaders and the elders, but they only led us astray.

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: And they will say, “Our Lord! We followed our chiefs and our elders – so they misled us from the path!”

67.author: Arberry
67.text: They shall say, 'Our Lord, we obeyed our chiefs and great ones, and they led us astray from the way.

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: And they will say: our Lord! verily we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they led us astray from the way.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: And they will say: "Our Lord! Verily, we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us from the (Right) Way.

67.author: Itani
67.text: And they will say, “Lord, we have obeyed our superiors and our dignitaries, but they led us away from the way.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: They will say: “Our Lord, we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they turned us away from the Right Way.

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: And they will say: "Our Lord! Verily, we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us from the way."

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: And they say: Our Lord! Lo! we obeyed our princes and great men, and they misled us from the Way.

67.author: Qarai
67.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord! We obeyed our leaders and elders, and they led us astray from the way.’

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: And they shall say: 'Our Lord, We obeyed our masters and our eminent ones, but they misled us from the way.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: And they will say, "Our Lord, indeed we obeyed our masters and our dignitaries, and they led us astray from the [right] way.

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: They will say, "Lord, we obeyed our chiefs and elders and they caused us to go astray.

67.author: Shakir
67.text: And they shall say: O our Lord! surely we obeyed our leaders and our great men, so they led us astray from the path;

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: They shall say, "Our Lord, we paid heed to our leaders and our elders, but they led us away from the right path.

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: And they would say: "Our Lord! We obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us as to the (right) Path.

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Waqaloo rabbana inna ataAAna sadatana wakubaraana faadalloona alssabeela

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: O Lord, give them a double punishment, and put a grievous curse upon them."

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: “Our Lord! Give them double the punishment of the fire and send upon them a mighty curse!”

68.author: Arberry
68.text: Our Lord, give them chastisement twofold, and curse them with a mighty curse!'

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: Our Lord! give them double torment and curse them with a great curse.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: Our Lord! give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse!"

68.author: Itani
68.text: Lord, give them double the punishment, and curse them with a great curse.”

68.author: Maududi
68.text: Our Lord, mete out to them a double chastisement and lay upon them a mighty curse.”

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: "Our Lord! Give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse!"

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: Our Lord! Oh, give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: Our Lord! Give them a double punishment and curse them with a mighty curse.’

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: Our Lord, let their punishment be doubled; and curse them with a mighty curse'

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: Our Lord, give them double the punishment and curse them with a great curse."

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: Lord, make them to suffer double torment and subject them to the greatest condemnation."

68.author: Shakir
68.text: O our Lord! give them a double punishment and curse them with a great curse.

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: Our Lord, give them double punishment and curse them with a mighty curse."

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: "Our Lord! Give them double Penalty and curse them with a very great Curse!"

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Rabbana atihim diAAfayni mina alAAathabi wailAAanhum laAAnan kabeeran

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: O you who believe, do not be like those who maligned Moses, whilst God cleared him of what they alleged; and he was held in high esteem with God.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: O People who Believe! Do not be like the people who troubled Moosa – so Allah freed him from the allegations they had uttered; and Moosa is honourable in the sight of Allah.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: O believers, be not as those who hurt Moses, but God declared him quit of what they said, and he was high honoured with God.

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: O Ye who believe! be not like unto those who annoyed Musa; but Allah cleared him of that which they said, and he was in the sight of Allah illustrious.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: O you who believe! Be not like those who annoyed Musa (Moses), but Allah cleared him of that which they alleged, and he was honourable before Allah.

69.author: Itani
69.text: O you who believe! Do not be like those who abused Moses; but God cleared him of what they said. He was distinguished with God.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: Believers, do not be like those who distressed Moses and then Allah declared him quit of the ill they spoke about him; and he had a high standing with Allah.

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: O you who believe! Be not like those who annoyed Musa, but Allah cleared him of that which they alleged, and he was honorable before Allah.

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: O ye who believe! Be not as those who slandered Moses, but Allah proved his innocence of that which they alleged, and he was well esteemed in Allah's sight.

69.author: Qarai
69.text: O you who have faith! Do not be like those who offended Moses, whereat Allah cleared him of what they alleged, and he was distinguished in Allah’s sight.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: Believers, do not be like those who hurt Moses. Allah cleared him of what they said. His face is honorable with Allah.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: O you who have believed, be not like those who abused Moses; then Allah cleared him of what they said. And he, in the sight of Allah, was distinguished.

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Believers, do not be like those who annoyed Moses. God proved him to be innocent of what they had said about him. Moses was a honorable person in the sight of God.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: O you who believe! be not like those who spoke evil things of Musa, but Allah cleared him of what they said, and he was worthy of regard with Allah.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Believers, do not behave like those who slandered Moses. God cleared him of their allegations. He was honourable in the sight of God.

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: O ye who believe! Be ye not like those who vexed and insulted Moses, but Allah cleared him of the (calumnies) they had uttered: and he was honourable in Allah's sight.

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo la takoonoo kaallatheena athaw moosa fabarraahu Allahu mimma qaloo wakana AAinda Allahi wajeehan

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: O you who believe, obey the commands of God, and say straightforward things

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: O People who Believe! Fear Allah, and speak rightly.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: O believers, fear God, and speak words hitting the mark,

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: O Ye who believe! fear Allah, and speak a straight speech.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: O you who believe! Keep your duty to Allah and fear Him, and speak (always) the truth.

70.author: Itani
70.text: O you who believe! Be conscious of God, and speak in a straightforward manner.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: Believers, fear Allah and speak the truth:

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: O you who believe! Have Taqwa of Allah and speak (always) the truth.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: O ye who believe! Guard your duty to Allah, and speak words straight to the point;

70.author: Qarai
70.text: O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and speak upright words.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: O you who believe, fear Allah and say sound statements,

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: Believers, have fear of God and speak righteous words.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: O you who believe! be careful of (your duty to) Allah and speak the right word,

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: Believers, fear God, and say the right word.

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: O ye who believe! Fear Allah, and (always) say a word directed to the Right:

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo ittaqoo Allaha waqooloo qawlan sadeedan

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: That He may straighten your affairs for you and forgive your sins; and he who obeys God and His Prophet will be successful.

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: He will rectify your deeds for you and forgive you your sins; and whoever obeys Allah and His Noble Messenger, has indeed achieved a great success.

71.author: Arberry
71.text: and He will set right your deeds for you and will forgive you your sins. Whosoever obeys God and His Messenger has won a mighty triumph.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: He WiLL rectify for you your works, and forgive you your sins. And Whosoever obeyeth Allah and His apostle, he hath indeed achieved a mighty achievement.

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: He will direct you to do righteous good deeds and will forgive you your sins. And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (SAW) he has indeed achieved a great achievement (i.e. he will be saved from the Hell-fire and made to enter Paradise).

71.author: Itani
71.text: He will rectify your conduct for you, and will forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger has won a great victory.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: Allah will set your deeds right for you and will forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has achieved a great triumph.

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: He will direct you to do righteous good deeds and will forgive you your sins. And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, he has indeed achieved a great victory.

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: He will adjust your works for you and will forgive you your sins. Whosoever obeyeth Allah and His messenger, he verily hath gained a signal victory.

71.author: Qarai
71.text: He will rectify your conduct for you and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle will certainly achieve a great success.

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: and He will mend your deeds for you and forgive your sins. Whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger shall win a great victory.

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: He will [then] amend for you your deeds and forgive you your sins. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has certainly attained a great attainment.

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: God will reform your deeds and forgive your sins. One who obeys God and His Messenger will certainly achieve a great success.

71.author: Shakir
71.text: He will put your deeds into a right state for you, and forgive you your faults; and whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, he indeed achieves a mighty success.

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: He will bless your works for you and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger has indeed achieved a great success.

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: That He may make your conduct whole and sound and forgive you your sins: He that obeys Allah and His Messenger, has already attained the highest achievement.

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Yuslih lakum aAAmalakum wayaghfir lakum thunoobakum waman yutiAAi Allaha warasoolahu faqad faza fawzan AAatheeman

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: We had offered the Trust (of divine responsibilities) to the heavens, the earth, the mountains, but they refrained from bearing the burden and were frightened of it; but man took it on himself. He is a faithless ignoramus.

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: We indeed offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused it and were afraid of it – and man accepted it; indeed he is one who puts himself into hardship, is extremely unwise.

72.author: Arberry
72.text: We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to carry it and were afraid of it; and man carried it. Surely he is sinful, very foolish.

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Verily We! We offered the trust unto the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it and shrank therefor. And man bore it; verily he was very iniquitous and very ignorant-

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: Truly, We did offer AlAmanah (the trust or moral responsibility or honesty and all the duties which Allah has ordained) to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they declined to bear it and were afraid of it (i.e. afraid of Allah's Torment). But man bore it. Verily, he was unjust (to himself) and ignorant (of its results).

72.author: Itani
72.text: We offered the Trust to the heavens, and the earth, and the mountains; but they refused to bear it, and were apprehensive of it; but the human being accepted it. He was unfair and ignorant.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to carry it and were afraid of doing so; but man carried it. Surely he is wrong-doing, ignorant.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: Truly, We did offer the Amanah to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they declined to bear it and were afraid of it. But man bore it. Verily, he was unjust and ignorant.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: Lo! We offered the trust unto the heavens and the earth and the hills, but they shrank from bearing it and were afraid of it. And man assumed it. Lo! he hath proved a tyrant and a fool.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: Indeed We presented the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to undertake it and were apprehensive of it; but man undertook it. Indeed he is most unjust and ignorant.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: We offered the trust to the heavens, and the earth, and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, and were afraid of it, and the human carried it. Surely, he is a harmdoer, and ignorant.

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: Indeed, we offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, and they declined to bear it and feared it; but man [undertook to] bear it. Indeed, he was unjust and ignorant.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: We offered Our Trust (Our deputation) to the heavens, to the earth, and to the mountains, but they could not bear this burden and were afraid to accept it. Mankind was able to accept this offer but he was unjust to himself and ignorant of the significance of this Trust.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: Surely We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to be unfaithful to it and feared from it, and man has turned unfaithful to it; surely he is unjust, ignorant;

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: We offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, because they were afraid of it. But man bore it: he surely proved unjust and ignorant.

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: We did indeed offer the Trust to the Heavens and the Earth and the Mountains; but they refused to undertake it, being afraid thereof: but man undertook it;- He was indeed unjust and foolish;-

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Inna AAaradna alamanata AAala alssamawati waalardi waaljibali faabayna an yahmilnaha waashfaqna minha wahamalaha alinsanu innahu kana thalooman jahoolan

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: So that God punishes men and women hypocrites, the idolaters and idolatrous women, but He turns to faithful men and women in forgiveness, for God is forgiving and kind.

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: In order that Allah may punish the hypocrite men, and the hypocrite women, and the polytheist men, and the polytheist women – and accept the repentance of believing men and believing women; and Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: That God may chastise the hypocrites, men and women alike, and the idolaters, men and women alike; and that God may turn again unto the believers, men and women alike. God is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: So that Allah will torment the hypocritical men and the hypocritical women and the associators and the associatoresses and Allah will relent toward believing men and the believing women; and Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: So that Allah will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the men and women who are AlMushrikun (the polytheists, idolaters, pagans, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW). And Allah will pardon (accept the repentance of) the true believers of the Islamic Monotheism, men and women. And Allah is Ever OftForgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Itani
73.text: God will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the idolaters, men and women. And God will redeem the believers, men and women. God is Ever-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: (The consequence of man's carrying the trust is) that Allah may chastise hypocritical men and hypocritical women and accept the repentance of believing men and believing women. He is Most Forgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: So that Allah will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the men and women who are idolators. And Allah will pardon the believers, men and women. And Allah is Ever Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: So Allah punisheth hypocritical men and hypocritical women, and idolatrous men and idolatrous women. But Allah pardoneth believing men and believing women, and Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.

73.author: Qarai
73.text: Allah will surely punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the polytheists, men and women, and Allah will turn clemently to the faithful, men and women, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: Allah punishes the hypocrites, men and women alike, and the idolaters, both men and women alike; and Allah turns to the believers, men and women alike. Allah is the Forgiver, the Most Merciful.

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: [It was] so that Allah may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women and the men and women who associate others with Him and that Allah may accept repentance from the believing men and believing women. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: (As a result of this) God will punish the hypocrites and the pagans, but He will accept the repentance of the believers. God is All-forgiving and All-merciful.

73.author: Shakir
73.text: So Allah will chastise the hypocritical men and the hypocritical women and the polytheistic men and the polytheistic women, and Allah will turn (mercifully) to the believing women, and Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: God will punish the hypocrites, both men and women, and polytheists, both men and women, but God will turn in His mercy to believing men and believing women; God is most forgiving and most merciful.

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: (With the result) that Allah has to punish the Hypocrites, men and women, and the Unbelievers, men and women, and Allah turns in Mercy to the Believers, men and women: for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: LiyuAAaththiba Allahu almunafiqeena waalmunafiqati waalmushrikeena waalmushrikati wayatooba Allahu AAala almumineena waalmuminati wakana Allahu ghafooran raheeman
